data.title: Atlas Home Health, founded by Aaron Starfire and Robert Okin in Napa, offers services for adults and children in Northern California.
data.description: Based in NAPA, Atlas Home Health has recently been launched to provide private client home healthcare services for adults and children in Northern California. The company was founded by Aaron Starfire, JD, and Robert Okin, MD, who are established healthcare figures and Napa residents. With Starfire's profound knowledge in home care delivery and health care navigation, and Dr. Okin's expertise as a renowned leader in mental health, Atlas Home Health aims to redefine excellence in home care services.

data.title: Inventor from NY creates STORK BOARD for maternity wards to keep families informed. - 70 characters
data.description: On July 24, 2025, an inventor from New York City launched a new innovation called the STORK BOARD in Pittsburgh. The inventor expressed the motivation behind this creation, aiming to provide updates for family and friends waiting in the maternity ward. The STORK BOARD enables visitors to monitor the status of their loved ones, creating a streamlined communication channel during the waiting period. This invention introduces a novel approach to keeping everyone informed and engaged during such important moments.

data.title: InventHelp Inventor from Cookeville, Tenn. develops SOLAR CAP for EVs to charge in sunlight. July 24 PRNewswire.
data.description: A resident of Cookeville, Tennessee, shared their innovative creation, the SOLAR CAP, aimed at simplifying the process of charging a vehicle battery when parked under the sun. Unveiled in Pittsburgh on July 24, 2025, the inventor expressed their motivation behind creating a practical solution for harnessing solar energy efficiently. The SOLAR CAP's straightforward application process and added protective benefits make it a convenient option for users seeking to recharge their batteries with ease.

data.title: Global surge in HbA1c testing for diabetes diagnosis highlighted in new 2025 market study from Kalorama Information.
data.description: In Arlington, Virginia on July 24, 2025, it was reported that the efforts to combat diabetes on a global scale are intensifying, with a focus on advancing diagnostic technologies. A recent market analysis conducted by Kalorama Information revealed significant insights in the report titled "Digital Diabetes: Global Market Opportunities in HbA1c Testing 2025". The study highlighted the increasing pace of advancements in diagnostic innovation for managing diabetes. This analysis delved into the global landscape, emphasizing the importance of HbA1c testing for diabetes diagnosis and monitoring.

data.title: $14M breach prompts WOO X trading platform to halt withdrawals for enhanced security after few user wallets were impacted.
data.description: A small portion of user wallets were compromised in the recent hack, prompting the team to suspend withdrawals temporarily to ensure further security measures.

data.title: Halal food market in MENA projected to witness significant growth due to rising demand and government support. Key hubs: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Egypt.
data.description: The halal food market in the MENA region is experiencing rapid growth due to increasing interest in ethical and health-conscious food choices, along with substantial government backing. Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Egypt are emerging as primary hubs in this sector. A recent study by BCC revealed these trends, indicating significant opportunities in this market. This surge is driven by a notable increase in demand for halal products, particularly in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Egypt. The supportive environment created by government policies has further fueled this expansion.

data.title: Cabometyx® approved in EU for previously treated advanced neuroendocrine tumors by European Commission on 24 July 2025.
data.description: On July 24th, 2025, it was disclosed that Ipsen has received approval from the European Commission for Cabometyx ® (cabozantinib). This medication has been sanctioned for use in treating adult individuals with unresectable or metastatic, well-differentiated pancreatic (pNET) and extra-pancreatic (epNET) neuroendocrine tumors. These patients must have experienced progression after undergoing a minimum of one prior systemic therapy, excluding somatostatin analogues. This approval marks a significant step in offering treatment options for this specific patient population in Europe.

data.title: Flex Ltd. (FLEX) stock down today despite beating estimates. Q1 earnings: 72 cents/share, sales: $6.575B (estimate: $6.263B). CEO optimistic about FY26.
data.description: Shares of Flex Ltd. (NASDAQ: FLEX) are declining today after the company reported its first-quarter earnings. The company exceeded expectations with adjusted earnings per share coming in at 72 cents, higher than the analyst consensus of 63 cents. Additionally, Flex reported quarterly sales of $6.575 billion, surpassing the Street's estimate of $6.263 billion. Flex's CEO, Revathi Advaithi, stated that these results showcase the company's strong focus on high-growth markets like data centers and power. This marks a positive start to FY26 for Flex. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Avangrid places 80th power generation project in operation; serves 23 states; adds 600 MW from 3 solar projects.
data.description: Avangrid, Inc., a prominent energy company under the Iberdrola Group, has successfully launched its 80th power generation project in the U.S. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to supplying energy to households and enterprises in 23 states nationwide. In the last half-year alone, Avangrid has unveiled three new solar projects in Texas, Ohio, and California, collectively contributing almost 600 megawatts (MW) to its power generation capacity.

data.title: Northern Trust (NASDAQ: NTRS) beats Q2 earnings with $2.13/share vs $2.01 est. Sales at $1.998B vs $1.955B. Stock up 3.7% to $128.77. Analysts revise price targets.
data.description: Northern Trust Corporation (NASDAQ: NTRS) announced positive second-quarter earnings, surpassing analyst expectations. The company reported earnings of $2.13 per share, exceeding the estimated $2.01 per share. Additionally, quarterly sales stood at $1.998 billion, beating the anticipated $1.955 billion. In response, Northern Trust shares increased by 3.7% and were trading at $128.77. Analysts adjusted their price targets for the company, with Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analyst David Konrad reiterating... Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CIQ appoints Peter Nelson, Apple and Claris veteran, as CTO to drive secure software innovation.
data.description: An experienced product and engineering executive has recently partnered with a leading open source organization to enhance secure and high-performance software advancements for the AI era. This collaboration was announced in Reno, Nevada on July 24, 2025. CIQ, the supporting services partner of Rocky Linux, known for its robust performance, is dedicated to driving innovation in the software realm.

data.title: Worldcoin (WLD) rises to $1.18 despite 2M daily token unlocks, supported by bullish MACD signals and strong $0.85 support amid regulatory challenges.
data.description: The cryptocurrency WLD is currently trading at $1.18 per token, despite daily unlocking of 2 million tokens. The token has demonstrated resilience, supported by bullish MACD signals, and maintaining strong support at $0.85. This performance comes in the face of regulatory challenges in the industry.

data.title: Teck Resources announces $0.125 dividend per share for Class A and B shares, to be paid on Sept 29, 2025.
data.description: Teck Resources Limited in Vancouver, British Columbia shared news that its Board of Directors has sanctioned a dividend of $0.125 per share for both Class A common shares and Class B subordinate voting shares. This dividend will be distributed to shareholders on September 29, 2025, for those recorded on September 15, 2025. Teck Resources Limited is listed under TSX as TECK.A and TECK.B, and on NYSE as TECK.

data.title: LA Auto Show opens registration for AutoMobility LA 2025, focusing on AI, EVs, and mobility policy. Show returns on Thursday, July 24th in Los Angeles.
data.description: Registration has begun for AutoMobility LA® 2025, the annual media and industry day of Los Angeles Auto Show®. The event, taking place on July 24, 2025, will highlight cutting-edge topics such as AI, electric vehicles (EVs), and mobility policy. Key focuses will include exploring advancements in AI technology and the growing EV market. AutoMobility LA® 2025 aims to gather industry leaders to discuss the future of transportation, with a strong emphasis on sustainability. This event is crucial for those in the automotive sector, with a significant number of attendees expected.
